rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Orders Granting Plaintiffs April 22, 2019 Ex Parte Application Effectively Denying The Borrowers Statutory Right Of Redemption As Void And Contrary To Law
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 8, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO VACATE ORDERS GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S APRIL 22, 2019 EX PARTE APPLICATION EFFECTIVELY DENYING THE BORROWER'S STATUTORY RIGHT OF REDEMPTION AS VOID AND CONTRARY TO LAW is DENIED. Nothing of the face of the "judgment roll" indicates that April 22, 2019 order is void. April 22, 2019 order concerned an unauthorized encumbrance in violation of a Court order; determination regarding stay requirements on appeal and authorization to obtain counsel. The order did not make determinations regarding right of redemption which is purportedly the basis for the moving party's argument that the order is void. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate April 22, 2019 Ex Parte Order On Grounds That The Motion Was Procedurally Defective Resulting In A Void Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 8, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE APRIL 22, 2019 EX PARTE ORDER ON GROUNDS THAT THE MOTION WAS PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE RESULTING IN A VOID ORDER is DENIED. There is nothing on the face of the "judgment roll" indicating that the judgment is void. On April 19, 2019 the ex-parte application was submitted for filing. The ex-parte calendar starts at 9:00 a.m. in the morning and parties are required to provide courtesy copies of their filings in support and in opposition (if any) to the Court. The Court hears the applications and reviews the courtesy copies. Apparently on April 22, 2019 a clerk of the Court reviewed and rejected the entirety of the filing due to formatting issues because a cover page was missing on the exhibits submitted for filing separately from the application itself. The formatting error was rectified in the re-submitted application and it appears on file with a May 7, 2019 filing date. There is no dispute that the application was submitted for filing and copies were provided to the Court and the parties in advance of April 22, 2019 hearing. All other arguments brought in this motion sound like a motion for reconsideration and do not support a finding of voidness (e.g. "the key declarations, were riddled with evidentiary insufficiencies, as the declarations were conclusory, based upon hearsay, and layered with suppositions."). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1367 NATOMA ST SF, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. GJ CREATIVE EQUITY GROUP LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 8, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address all issues raised by demurrer and to allege facts in support of each cause of action as to the moving defendant. Specifically (but not limited to) factual allegations shall connect the alleged wrongdoings by Gallin and Jones with any actives of real estate agents and brokers giving rise to fiduciary duties/contractual obligations of real estate agents/brokers, etc.. (Allegations in paragraph 12 of the Frist Amended Complaint fail to do so). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: CALVIN LI VS. FREDERICK PAGLIUCA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement (Ccp 664.6)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 8, 2021 line 7. PLAINTIFF CALVIN LI'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT (CCP 664.6) is DENIED without prejudice. No opposition filed. Plaintiff is seeking entry of judgment per November 29, 2018 Stipulation filed with the Court (Motion 1:26-27). No such Stipulation appears on file. For the purposes of this ruling the Court will assume that Plaintiff is seeking entry of judgment per October 10, 2017 Settlement Agreement filed with the Court and attached to Plaintiff's declaration. Plaintiff asserts entitlement to damages, costs and fees for breach of paragraph 3 of the Settlement Agreement. Plaintiff provides no evidence that defendant did not terminate his tenancy, did not vacate and did not surrender prior to the expiration of the probationary period (Settlement 2:19-23). Plaintiff further provides no authority or evidence of entitlement to fees ($800) and costs ($60) re: breach of paragraph 3 of the settlement agreement. (See paragraph 5 stipulating to fees and costs in the event of breach of paragraph 2 of the Settlement Agreement). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
